data.title: Retail reckoning: Party City is goes out of business after a 40-year run
data.description:
Party City Holdco Inc. (OTCPK:PRTYQ) is closing all stores and winding down operations immediately.

Party City CEO Barry Litwin told corporate employees that the company is winding down operations, and that December 20 was their last day of employment. "It's really important for you to know that we've done everything possible that we could to try to avoid this outcome," stated Litwin. "Unfortunately, it's necessary to commence a winddown process immediately," he added.

Party City was founded by Steve Mandell in 1986, with its first 4,000-square-foot store located in East Hanover, New Jersey. By 1990, Party City had incorporated as a franchising operation with 11 stores1. The chain experienced rapid growth throughout the early 1990s, reaching 58 stores by 1993. In 1996, Party City went public, and by the end of that year, the chain had reached 200 stores. Party City continued to grow over the next 25 years to expand its store base to over 850 company-owned and franchise outlets. The chain even expanded internationally by acquiring the Canadian retailer Party Packagers.

Party City's share price fell below $10 in July 2021 and never made it back to double-digits as losses started to mount up. In 2023, the New Jersey-based party supply chain filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection

Analyst think Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and Wayfair (W) are three retailers that could see a minor benefit with incremental sales due to the Party City collapse.

data.title: Principal Financial Group calls for market optimism in 2025
data.description:
With the Federal Reserve sending reverberations through Wall Street on Wednesday with a hawkish interest rate cut, a potential Santa rally has come under pressure, and the outlook for next year has been clouded somewhat.

Still, Principal Financial Group on Friday made a case for optimism in 2025. The financial institution believes U.S. economic growth is set to remain above trend next year, citing both a strong consumer and solid company fundamentals.

“As we move into 2025, the U.S. economy is set to remain a beacon of strength, upheld by strong consumers and corporate balance sheets,” Principal Financial Group said in an investor note.

PFG went on to say that as investors move into 2025, the three largest economies— the U.S., China, and the EU, which together account for almost 60% of global GDP— “are all signaling stimulative policy actions.” Barring disruptions from inflation or geopolitical tensions, this favorable liquidity environment is likely to bolster market performance according to PFG.

From a U.S. perspective, the incoming Trump administration is also seen as a reason for optimism as Wall Street may witness a push for broader deregulation and lower corporate and individual taxes.

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the markets, they can turn towards broader market tracking exchange-traded funds. Some ETFs worth noting are as follows: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (QQQM), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: OpenAI reveals new frontier model o3; says it crushes prior AI benchmarks
data.description:
OpenAI revealed its latest frontier models, o3 and o3-mini, which the startup said crushes benchmarks set by its prior artificial intelligence models.

"We have an exciting one for you today," said CEO Sam Altman during a livestream on Friday. "We are announcing o3 and o3-mini."

Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI skipped o2 as it went straight from o1 to o3, in order to prevent any confusion with 02, a British telecommunications service provider.

"We can use these models to do increasingly complex tasks," Altman said. "This model is incredible at programming."

"o3 is a strong model," said Mark Chen, head of multimodal and frontier research at OpenAI. "It achieves very strong scores in coding, math and PhD science questions. We are nearing saturation for a lot of these scores. We are going to have to set higher benchmarks for testing."

Greg Kamradt, president of ARC Prize, which created ARC-AGI, an AI benchmark, said the AI model that beats the ARC-AGI will be the one paving the path to general intelligence.

"It took five years for AI models to go from 0% to 5% scores on the ARC-AGI," Kamradt said. "o3 was able to score an 87.5%. This is new territory. Looking at these scores, I need to change my AI mindset, as this sets a new bar. I will be working with OpenAI next year to create a new benchmark."

The o3 mini also sets a new standard in cost-efficient AI, OpenAI said. It will feature low, medium and high models. The o3 mini medium is already better and cheaper for coding than the standard o1 model.

"We aren't publicly launching these today, but we are starting safety testing today," Altman said. "There is a new public testing phase we have added."

Members of the public can go to the OpenAI website and apply to be a safety tester. Applicants have until Jan. 10, 2025, to apply.

"We plan to release these to the public by the end of January," Altman added. "The more people that can help us test, the faster we can get it out."

The new frontier models from OpenAI look to compete with Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) new Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking.

"Built on 2.0 Flash's speed and performance, this model is trained to use thoughts to strengthen its reasoning," said Google DeepMind's chief scientist Jeff Dean, in a post on X on Thursday. "And we see promising results when we increase inference time computation!"

The announcement by OpenAI marked the final day of the 12 Days of OpenAI event. Prior announcements included a dial-in mode for ChatGPT, free ChatGPT Search; Projects with ChatGPT, the launch of Sora, an artificial intelligence-powered video generator that can create videos with as little as a few words of text; ChatGPT Canvas; Advanced Voice with video and screen sharing; and the integration of ChatGPT into Apple's latest operating system update.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow soar as soft inflation data helps overcome Fed 'overreaction'
data.description:
Wall Street surged on Friday, after some favorable inflation data helped traders look past the Federal Reserve's hawkish tilt that roiled markets two days ago.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +1.54% at 5,957.34 points in afternoon trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.55% at 19,673.68 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1.55% at 42,998.01 points.

All 11 S&P sectors advanced, led by Real Estate and Technology.

A spree of bond-buying sent U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) snapped a two-day selloff, slipping 6 basis points to 4.51%. The shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) shed 2 basis points to 4.31%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

U.S. stocks tried to rebound in Thursday's session following Wednesday's Fed-inspired carnage. However, due to a lack of any major catalysts for positive sentiment, the attempt largely fizzled out.

Friday's economic calendar provided the needed spark. Before the opening bell, market participants received U.S. personal income and outlays data for November. That report contained an update on the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index - widely seen as the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. In November, the core PCE rose 0.1% M/M, a slower pace than the consensus of +0.2% and October's gain of 0.3%.

"If Fed policymakers were making wishes, they'd want services spending to cool, goods spending to pick up and for all of this to happen with inflation coming down and a labor market strong enough to sustain income growth. Today's personal income and spending report largely granted those wishes," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said.

The Fed on Wednesday had cited inflation as one of its primary reasons for forecasting a slower pace of interest rate cuts. Fed chair Jerome Powell even went on to say that policymakers' year-end projection for inflation had "fallen apart" into the end of 2024. Following such messaging, today's soft core PCE deflator reading came as a balm to investors.

Princeton economics professor and former Fed vice chair Alan Blinder called the Fed-induced selling a "way, way overreaction."

"Many years ago, I coined the aphorism that in calm markets, they generally react three times as much as they should, and in panicky markets they react ten times as much. This looked like a ten to me," Blinder said in a Bloomberg TV interview on Wednesday.

On Friday, politics grabbed a chunk of the spotlight. A stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown. Trump called for a removal of the debt ceiling.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Turning to Friday's active stocks, U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) slumped -17%, after the Danish drugmaker announced disappointing results from a late-stage trial of its next-gen weight loss therapy, CagriSema. Rival Eli Lilly (LLY) climbed +5%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on the markets

  • We Are Carving Out A Bottom, Don't Panic
  • 2025 Markets To Be Driven By Liquidity, Lobbying And Collectibility - Most Likely
  • Canadian Banks Are Seeing Increased Risks
  • As Trump aims to slash federal spending, Wells Fargo economists see no major cuts in 2025
  • J.P. Morgan’s top Financial stocks for 2025

data.title: CVC looking for partners for potential ITV takeover - report
data.description:
Private equity firm CVC Capital Partners is evaluating teaming up for a potential ITV Plc (OTCPK:ITVPF) bid.

The PE shop is seeking a partner to split ITV's studio and broadcasting businesses, according to a Dealreporter item on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

CVC has sounded out Sony Corp. (SONY) and MediaforEurope for a potential purchase of ITV (OTCPK:ITVPF), the report said. Sony would likely want the ITV Studios, while MFE would be interested in the broadcasting segment.

Talks were held in recent weeks and deliberations are still at an early stage, according to the report.

data.title: Big Lots 'going out of business' a huge opportunity for Ollie's Bargain Outlet
data.description:
The “Going Out of Business” sale at Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) could be a huge opportunity for Ollie’s Bargain Outlet (NASDAQ:OLLI) to scoop up market share and real estate at bargain prices. Piper Sandler’s Peter Keith sees near-term headwinds to OLLI's comparable sales from Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) close-out sales but expects a “significant” lift in sales in 2025 from a full liquidation.

Keith also sees a benefit to Ollie’s (OLLI) from the real estate left empty by Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ), enabling competitors in an abnormally strong negotiating position for these sites.

“While Ollie shares have rallied 22% since the Q3 print last week, we believe significant upside remains as a Big Lots liquidation would represent the greatest market share and store opportunity for OLLI in its 40+ year history,” Keith writes in Friday’s research note.

The initial drag on OLLI comparable sales will be seen in Q4 and Q1 2025 with roughly 800 OLLI stores impacted, resulting in a headwind of as much as 3% to 4% spread across the two quarters. But on the other end, with ~900 Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) closures overlapping with existing OLLI stores, Keith estimates a +7% comparable sales lift to OLLI in 2025 from share capture.

Piper Sandler assigns an Overweight rating to Ollie’s Bargain Outlook (OLLI) and $126 price target, which assumes a 7% upside for the stock.

data.title: Ares Management, Tradeweb upgraded to Outperform at KBW
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods upgraded Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) and Tradeweb Markets (NASDAQ:TW) to Outperform as KBW expects a favorable outlook for capital markets in 2025, with an expectation that the sector will generate close to ~10% total returns.

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) stock climbed 2.9%, and Tradeweb (NASDAQ:TW) gained 2.7% in Friday afternoon trading.

For retail brokers, analyst Kyle Voigt raised estimates due to a higher interest rate forecast, signs of growth in sweep cash balances, and a supportive equity market backdrop. For exchanges, KBW said valuations are reasonable, while companies still offer solid downside protection with defensive growth. For alternative asset managers, the firm sees a favorable fundamental outlook and beneficial cyclical factors, such as credit spreads being near 17-year lows.

Voigt sees a higher base rate forecast and resilient economic growth outlook strengthening institutional/retail demand for credit products in 2025, prompting him to upgrade Ares (ARES) from Market Perform.

"We don’t think the recent shift in the yield curve will be enough to derail a return of M&A activity in 2025, and instead continue to expect a sharp rebound in M&A volumes — which should help drive better net deployment trends for ARES in 2025 and should also help to create a better supply/demand balance in the direct lending market more broadly," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Tradeweb (TW) stands to benefit from higher-for-longer rates boosting rates activity, share gains in the cash Treasury, its partnership with BlackRock's (BLK) Aladdin platform, and strength in emerging markets swaps.

"TW's current FY3 multiple of 35.6X is still a material 18% discount to valuations reached in 2H21 during ZIRP (zero interest rate policy)," Voigt wrote. "Overall, we don't see TW's multiple as over-stretched here,and believe it could hold as long as double-digit organic revenue growth persists into 2026 (which we currently expect with organic revenue growth of ~11%)."

KBW's Outperform rating on Ares (ARES) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and agrees with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

Similarly, the SA Quant system has a Hold rating on Tradeweb (TW), in contrast to the average Wall Street rating of buy.

data.description:
• Granite Ridge Resources (NYSE:GRNT) rose 6% after a report that Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) has made an offer for the shale oil producer.
  • Northern Oil (NOG) has submitted at least two bids for Granite Ridge (GRNT), according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The latest offer was at a 20% premium to GRNT's share price.

data.title: Five reasons why UBS is overweight the UK
data.description:
UBS analysts said they are overweight the United Kingdom (NYSEARCA:EWU) in their Outlook 2025 report.

Andrew Garthwaite, chief global equity strategist at UBS, named five reasons why:

The UK (EWU) outperforms when defensive sectors outperform. Here, UBS analysts explain why they prefer defensives over cyclicals.

Europe (VGK), (IEV), (IEUR) remains “abnormally cheap” on sector-adjusted price-over-earnings. “Nearly all sectors (except for commercial services and luxury) are trading on higher-than-normal discounts,” said Garthwaite. “The buyback yield in the UK (as percentage of market cap) is much higher than the U.S. and it used to be the other way around.”

The UK benefits from a stronger dollar because nearly 20% of revenues come from the U.S. UBS analysts forecasts the dollar to slowly appreciate by end 2026, although not in 2025.

The UK 10-year/Germany 10-year government bond spread “seems to have moved too far,” Garthwaite said. The UK expected inflation rate over 2030-2035 is at 3.4%.

Lastly, “domestic factors look better than elsewhere,” he said. UBS forecasts an above consensus for 2025 UK GDP growth at 1.5% (consensus is at 1.4%). “The UK particularly benefits from strong real wage growth (currently 2.5%, but likely to slow), a pick-up in employment (UBS forecasts 1.4% employment growth in 2025), and fiscal easing (1.1% of GDP).”

It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

data.title: Texas Instruments finalizes $1.6B CHIPs Act award
data.description:
Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) announced on Friday that it has finalized the $1.6B award from the Biden administration's $53B CHIPs Act to help it expand its plants in Texas and Utah.

The grants will be used to support three new 300 millimeter semiconductor wafer fabs already under construction, the company said in a statement. Unlike cutting edge chips used for artificial intelligence and other high-end technologies, these are considered "legacy" chips, used in a variety of consumer devices.

Texas Instruments also said it expects to receive between $6B and $8B from the U.S. Treasury's Investment Tax Credit for "qualified U.S. manufacturing investments" and up to $10M for workforce development.

The new plants — which will be located in Sherman, Texas and Lehi, Utah — will create roughly 2,000 new jobs, Texas Instruments said.

The award was first announced in August.

"As the largest analog and embedded processing semiconductor manufacturer in the U.S., TI is uniquely positioned to provide dependable, low-cost 300mm semiconductor manufacturing capacity at scale," said Texas Instruments President and CEO Haviv Ilan. "The increasing number of electronic devices in our lives depend on our foundational chips, and we appreciate the support from the U.S. government to make the semiconductor ecosystem stronger and more resilient."

Shares of the analog chip supplier rose 2% in midday trading.
